rationale: Hard pass for TheSphinx. The dancing-teen UGC archetype skews Gen-Z casual-puzzle (Crossword Go reference) and clashes directly with the 25-45 educated-adult, "mystical minimalism" premium positioning — the blue/olive/cream palette and goofy emotional tone undermine the dark-gold mysterious brand. Mechanically there's zero way to convey the voice-based yes/no deduction loop through a wordless dance hook; if tested at all, rebuild as a sophisticated POV UGC where an adult interrogates the AI and reacts to nailing a celebrity guess.
